name: Old Pochmann (3BLD)
description: The standard beginner method for 3x3 blindfolded solving (3BLD), created by Stefan Pochmann. Old Pochmann (OP) uses a simple two-piece swapping technique to solve edges and corners one at a time. The solver memorizes the positions of all pieces, then executes a series of setup moves followed by a single swap algorithm (T-perm for edges, Y-perm for corners) to place each piece. Its simplicity and reliability make it the most recommended first 3BLD method.

steps.name: Memorization (Edges)
steps.description: Memorize the cycle of all 12 edges. Starting from a buffer position (typically the UF or DF edge), trace where each piece needs to go, forming a sequence of letters or images. Each edge sticker is assigned a letter, and the solver traces the cycle: buffer goes to target, target's occupant becomes the next target, and so on until the cycle closes. Multiple cycles may be needed.
tips: Use a lettering scheme (e.g., Speffz) where each sticker has a letter A-X
Practice letter-pair memorization using images or words
Start with UF or DF as your edge buffer
Keep track of solved and unsolved pieces carefully
Memo edges first, then corners (edges are harder to retain)

steps.name: Memorization (Corners)
steps.description: Memorize the cycle of all 8 corners using the same letter-tracing technique. Starting from a buffer position (typically the UFR or UBL corner), trace the corner cycles. Corners have three stickers each, so the letter assigned depends on which sticker faces which direction.
tips: UBL or UFR are common corner buffer positions
Practice distinguishing corner orientations (which sticker to read)
Use letter pairs combined with a story or journey method for memo

steps.name: Execution (Corners)
steps.description: Solve all corners by executing the memorized sequence. For each target in the memo, perform a setup move to bring the target piece to a specific position, execute the Y-perm (or another corner-swapping algorithm), then undo the setup move. This swaps the buffer piece with the target piece. Repeat for each letter in the corner memo.
tips: Y-perm is the standard corner swap algorithm
Setup moves bring the target to the swap position without disturbing the buffer
Practice setup moves until they are automatic
Execute corners first so edge setup moves do not disturb solved corners (in OP/OP approach)

steps.name: Execution (Edges)
steps.description: Solve all edges using the same setup-swap-undo technique. For each target in the edge memo, perform setup moves to bring the target edge to a specific position, execute the T-perm (or another edge-swapping algorithm), then reverse the setup moves. Repeat for the entire edge memo sequence.
tips: T-perm is the standard edge swap algorithm for Old Pochmann
Setup moves must preserve already-solved corners if doing corners first
Practice the setup moves for all 22 edge sticker positions
Some positions require only M-slice or U/D setup moves

pros: Simplest 3BLD execution method — only 2 algorithms needed (T-perm and Y-perm)
Very reliable and easy to understand
Good foundation for understanding BLD solving concepts
cons: Very high move count (100-200+ total moves)
Slow execution compared to M2/R2 or 3-style
Execution phase can be tediously long
creator: Stefan Pochmann
